prompts.seed:
Classify the given text into one of these categories: alarm, apr, balance, book_flight, book_hotel, calculator, calories, car_rental, cook_time, credit_limit, credit_score, definition, direct_deposit, freeze_account, lost_luggage, meeting_schedule, pay_bill, payday, pto_request, recipe, report_fraud, report_lost_card, restaurant_reservation, restaurant_reviews, rewards_balance, taxes, timer, transfer, travel_suggestion, weather.

Respond with exactly one category name from the list above and nothing else.
prompts.x1:
Classify the given text into exactly one of the following categories: alarm, apr, balance, book_flight, book_hotel, calculator, calories, car_rental, cook_time, credit_limit, credit_score, definition, direct_deposit, freeze_account, lost_luggage, meeting_schedule, pay_bill, payday, pto_request, recipe, report_fraud, report_lost_card, restaurant_reservation, restaurant_reviews, rewards_balance, taxes, timer, transfer, travel_suggestion, weather.

Definitions and rules for difficult distinctions:
- pto_request: Requests or statements about taking paid time off, vacation, leave from work, or asking about personal days. Includes intent to request, plan, or check on time off.
- travel_suggestion: Asking for ideas or recommendations for travel destinations, tips, or trip planning not explicitly as time off from work.
  - If the text is about wanting or planning a vacation/leave and could fit pto_request (especially if implied to be work-related), prefer pto_request; only use travel_suggestion if explicitly seeking travel options/destinations/plans.
- payday: Questions about the timing or date of salary or wage payment, such as "when is payday?" or "when do I get paid?"
- direct_deposit: Questions about payment methods, status, or setup of direct deposit (e.g., "has my salary been deposited via direct deposit?", "set up direct deposit"). 
  - For questions about the date of receiving salary/wages, use payday.  
  - For questions about *how* or *whether* a payment was deposited, use direct_deposit.
If a text could refer to both pto_request and travel_suggestion, or both payday and direct_deposit, select pto_request or payday, respectively.

Respond with exactly one category name from the list above and nothing else.
prompts.x2:
Classify the given text into exactly one of the following categories: alarm, apr, balance, book_flight, book_hotel, calculator, calories, car_rental, cook_time, credit_limit, credit_score, definition, direct_deposit, freeze_account, lost_luggage, meeting_schedule, pay_bill, payday, pto_request, recipe, report_fraud, report_lost_card, restaurant_reservation, restaurant_reviews, rewards_balance, taxes, timer, transfer, travel_suggestion, weather.

Definitions and decision rules for fine distinctions:
- book_hotel: Requests or queries about finding, booking, reserving, or evaluating hotels, motels, or other lodging. If the text is seeking accommodation, or reviews specifically for hotels, select book_hotel.
  - If the text asks for reviews or likes specifically about hotels (not restaurants or general attractions), choose book_hotel, even if the word "reviews" appears.
- restaurant_reviews: Questions or interest in feedback, opinions, or ratings specifically about restaurants or places to eat.
- direct_deposit: Questions about setting up, activating, confirming, or inquiring about the status or method of direct deposit (e.g., "how do I get paid direct deposit," "is my payment in my account via direct deposit").
  - For texts about how employees are paid or about payment methods (not payment dates or making payments), select direct_deposit over pay_bill.
- pay_bill: Requests or instructions to pay a bill, make a payment, or send money for a debt or invoice.
- payday: Questions about when one gets paid, salary/wage deposit dates, or company pay schedules.
- pto_request: Statements about taking time off, vacation, leave, or checking/requesting personal days (work-related).
- travel_suggestion: Asking for travel ideas, destinations, or trip recommendations, where time off from work is not implied.
- If a text could fit both pto_request and travel_suggestion, or both payday and direct_deposit, or both book_hotel and restaurant_reviews, prioritize in this order:
  1. pto_request over travel_suggestion
  2. payday over direct_deposit
  3. book_hotel over restaurant_reviews (if accommodation is the focus)
- For any text about lodging, finding a hotel, or wanting a hotel with good reviews, select book_hotel, not restaurant_reviews.
- For any text about payment methods or how salaries are delivered (not *paying* an expense), select direct_deposit, not pay_bill.

Respond with exactly one category name from the list above and nothing else.
